what_it_does: Records metadata for a single channel attachment: an image in the design-assets channel, identified by a UUID and linked to a target image URI with an addition timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content
standouts: The target filename 'image (14)' contains a space and a numeric suffix, suggesting an auto-generated or duplicate name
The file is a minimal metadata stub with no descriptive caption or dimensions
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured ledger metadata
File is named att-<uuid>.md under channel-memory/channel_attachments/
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
concerns: Target filename is generic and may be hard to identify semantically
No alt text, dimensions, MIME type, or checksum are recorded
No indication of which message or user added the attachment
notes: A bare attachment ledger stub that registers an image asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: What does the '(14)' suffix in the target filename signify?
Is 'image (14)' a duplicate or auto-renamed file?
Which channel message or user does this attachment belong to?
Is the image:// URI resolvable by the current storage system?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's path and fields imply it should catalog a channel attachment; it does exactly that, though with very sparse metadata.
intention_signals: To create a durable, queryable record that links a channel attachment entry to its underlying stored image asset.
contribution: Provides a canonical, timestamped identifier and target pointer for a design-assets image attachment, supporting retrieval, audit, and cross-referencing independent of the channel's runtime storage.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger stub that records metadata for an image attachment (id att-f5218337) in the design-assets channel, pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (14) and noting its addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to catalog and preserve a reference to a specific image asset attached to the design-assets channel.
